title: The Forty Days
subtitle: Christ lingers with his own, proving the Resurrection true, before the Ascension

stillness: Be still, and let the noise of the world fall away.
The Lord no longer dwells continually among the crowd.
Wait quietly, as the disciples waited, for the One who comes and goes.
gospel: He showed himself alive after his Passion.
appearing to them through forty days,
and speaking of the kingdom of God.
behold: Behold the kindness, the love, and the humility of the Lord,
which, as ever, shine out in all his deeds.
Though he had now gloriously conquered and risen,
still he willed to wander among them forty days more,
to confirm and strengthen his disciples.
After so many years, so many labors and afflictions,
after a death so cruel, so shameful, so bitter,
the conqueror could rightly have returned at once to his glory.
But his love would not allow it.
He willed to dwell with them in person.
In his own body he showed the scars,
though all disfigurement of a scar is utterly foreign to an immortal body.
He ate with them and drank,
though a spiritual body needs no nourishment of food.
He ate, then, after the Resurrection by power, not by need.
And perhaps the holy Fathers themselves, Abraham above all, and David,
came with him to look upon that most excellent daughter of theirs,
the Mother of the Lord, who found grace and bore the Redeemer.
O how gladly they gazed upon her,
how reverently they bowed before her,
though they were not seen by those who stood there in the body.
consider: He did not rise straightway to heaven.
He delayed, to prove the Resurrection in many ways,
to comfort and revive his disciples,
for we too must wait with even hearts for our reward.
As there were forty hours of his death,
so for forty days he dwelt with them after the Resurrection,
for divine consolation surpasses human sorrow.
He did this for them, and for us; but we do not heed it.
He loved, and he loves, so vehemently, and is not loved in return,
though at such a fire we ought not only to be warmed, but to burn.
He showed himself not to the wicked, nor to lovers of the world,
but to his Apostles alone and the other faithful.
For it was not fitting that Christ, risen from the dead,
should show himself to wicked men, dead to God,
cut off from the Life which Christ himself is.
He appeared, then, to witnesses foreordained.
prayer: in which, living and immortal, you appeared to your disciples and faithful;
by the most gracious lingering and the sweetest conversations
which you had with them through forty days,
appearing to them with many proofs and speaking of the kingdom of God,
strengthening them, and eating with them,
that they might prove and believe the true Resurrection in you,
and you took all doubt away from their hearts:
I beg you, good and merciful Jesus,
grant me, among the witnesses foreordained by God,
to become a witness of your Resurrection,
not by tongue and word alone,
but by deed, and in truth. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Dilexit, et diligit sic vehementer et non diligitur, cum tamen ad talem et tantum ignem non solum calefieri, sed etiam ardere deberemus.
latinJewel.english: He loved, and he loves, so vehemently, and yet is not loved in return — though at such and so great a fire we ought not only to be warmed, but even to burn.
